subject: fix(metrics,runway): timezone bug in 30-day filter + missing initial balance point
body:
MetricsGrid: toISOString().slice(0,10) returns UTC date, not local.
For users east/west of UTC the 30-day expense window was off by 1 day.
Now uses formatDate() which outputs local-timezone YYYY-MM-DD.

Runway: dailyBalance array now includes the starting balance as the
first point (day before forecast begins). Previously the graph had no
reference point for where the balance started — the line began at the
first day's change, making the projection harder to interpret.

Tags: metrics, runway, timezone, graph
Complexity: 2
Urgency: 3
Impact: user-facing
Risk: low
Debt: paid
Epic: stability
Semver: patch

title: timezone bug in 30-day filter + missing initial balance point
